plainEnglish: Set the page's default language with a lang attribute on the <html> element (e.g., lang="en"). Screen readers use it to choose the right pronunciation rules and voice; without it, English text might be read with a French accent and vice versa.
whyItMatters: Screen readers mispronounce an entire page—making it hard to understand—when the <html> element has no valid lang attribute telling them which language to speak.
quickCheck: Inspect the <html> element and confirm it has a valid lang attribute (e.g., lang="en"); axe's html-has-lang and html-lang-valid rules check this.
commonMistakes: Omitting the lang attribute on the <html> element entirely (axe html-has-lang).
Using an invalid or made-up language code like lang="english" instead of lang="en" (axe html-lang-valid).
lang and xml:lang attributes that disagree on the page's language (axe html-xml-lang-mismatch).
Hard-coding lang="en" on a site that serves multiple languages instead of setting it per locale.
